title: How Long Can This Tale of Deception Continue?
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The Tamil below is a source-checked reading copy: the opening salutation omitted by OCR has been restored; dates, project capacities and cost figures have been retained; and clear OCR character and word-break errors have been repaired. The canonical source file remains unchanged and authoritative. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: Speaking in the Tamil Nadu Assembly, present Chief Minister O. Panneerselvam broadly declared, without specific supporting statistics, that projects completed since the Jayalalithaa-led AIADMK Government took office in 2011 and medium- and long-term purchases now provide 4,640 MW of electricity.

The Governor's Address on 17 February 2015 states on page 25: “The State's total generating capacity has increased by 3,358 MW over the past four years.” DMDK opposition MLA Mohanraj said that when he sought generation details under the Right to Information Act, he learned that not even one megawatt had been generated by a new AIADMK project after the party took office. Newspapers too have written with evidence that the AIADMK Government has not conceived, completed and generated even one megawatt from a new project. Yet the Government has not come forward to state the truth.
Speaking in Coimbatore on 15 September 2014, Jayalalithaa claimed: “In the past three years alone, 2,793 MW of additional installed capacity has been added.”

Responding on 21 February 2015 to Panneerselvam's Assembly claim of a 4,640-MW increase, PMK founder Dr Ramadoss explained: “None of these was implemented by the AIADMK Government. This additional electricity came from projects implemented under the previous DMK Government and through joint ventures with Central public-sector power companies.”

Panneerselvam also said another 2,163 MW would arrive in the next few months. Dr Ramadoss replied that this too was not due to the AIADMK and that most listed projects had been announced before 2010, during DMK rule. He added:

“Like showing a crying child a banana to quiet it, the AIADMK Government offers such statistics to pacify people suffering from power cuts. It takes pride in announcing vast power schemes but shows no interest in implementing them. Buying electricity from private companies will only open the way to corruption.”

Panneerselvam said environmental clearance for the 660-MW Ennore Thermal Power Project was granted by the Union Government on 24 January 2013; technical-commercial bids for procurement and construction were opened on 15 March 2013; price bids followed on 12 August; and on 27 February 2014, the work order was awarded to Lanco Infratech Limited.
The Union Government granted approval two years ago. Why has generation still not begun? Is this Government not responsible for the delay?

In a Rule 110 statement in the Assembly on 29 March 2012, Jayalalithaa announced: “My Government has now decided to implement a new 660-MW Ennore Thermal Power expansion project. It will begin generation by the end of 2015.” Yet yesterday Panneerselvam told the same Assembly that construction is only now under way. How can the project begin generation by the end of 2015? Was Jayalalithaa's Rule 110 statement not false information intended to divert and deceive the people? Panneerselvam has made that clear by saying, in effect, “Jayalalithaa announced it, but construction is only now beginning.”

There is more comedy. Jayalalithaa said generation would begin in 2015. Electricity Minister Natham Viswanathan's statement of 29 December 2014 said the Ennore project would begin generation after forty-two months, in 2018. The present Chief Minister, apparently wishing to avoid trouble, gives no starting date at all and says only that the company has now begun construction.
Only truth can be told consistently. Everyone knows that lies produce contradictions. Even over electricity, the AIADMK Government piles cosmic falsehood upon sky-high falsehood.

Panneerselvam also said the Union Ministry of Environment and Forests recommended draft terms of reference for the 1,600-MW Uppur project on 28 May 2012; its detailed project report was finalised in January 2014; and it is expected to become operational in 2019-20. If a project cleared by the Union Government in 2012 is expected to operate only four years from now, can we not understand the AIADMK Government's commitment to completing it?

That is not all. In her Rule 110 statement of 29 March 2012, Jayalalithaa declared amid loud applause: “The pre-feasibility report and project plan for the 1,600-MW Uppur Thermal Power Project have been finalised. The project will become operational by the end of 2015.” Newspapers printed it in bold type. Three years later, Panneerselvam says it is expected only in 2019-20. Must we not ask whom they seek to deceive by repeatedly staging this drama of falsehood?

Speaking in the Assembly on 25 October 2013, Jayalalithaa said: “No member need worry. I state categorically that very soon, extremely soon, Tamil Nadu will be made a power-surplus State. We shall certainly achieve it.” The next month, campaigning in the Yercaud by-election on 28 November, she said: “The Union Government caused the power cuts. I shall successfully overcome even that situation, correct Tamil Nadu's shortage and very soon create a bright and prosperous condition without any power cut.”
Nothing Jayalalithaa promised was fulfilled. But she did deceive the people and gain publicity in the newspapers.

On solar power, Panneerselvam told the Assembly that since this Government took office in 2011, private parties had installed 112 MW of solar capacity now supplying the State. But what had this Government promised? Jayalalithaa announced that Tamil Nadu would lead in solar energy by generating 3,000 MW over three years: 1,000 MW in 2013, another 1,000 in 2014 and another 1,000 in 2015.

From an announced 3,000 MW, Panneerselvam now says 112 MW is available. The people of Tamil Nadu have begun to understand how this Government performs opportunistic feats and deceives them with impossible boasts: “We shall bend the sky into a bow and twist sand into rope.”

In its 2011 policy note, immediately after taking office, the AIADMK said four projects begun under the DMK would provide 3,228 MW by the end of 2012: North Chennai Stage II, Mettur Stage III, Vallur, a joint venture of the Tamil Nadu Electricity Board and NTPC, and Thoothukudi, a joint venture of the Board and Neyveli Lignite Corporation. Had the AIADMK shown proper concern in completing those four projects alone, Tamil Nadu would not have faced power cuts.

Nevertheless, since 2013, Tamil Nadu has received on average around 2,000 additional MW from these new plants begun under the DMK: 1,200 MW from the North Chennai expansion, 1,000 MW from Vallur and 600 MW from the Mettur expansion. This allowed the AIADMK Government to announce that power restrictions would be withdrawn from June 2014. Wind generation then suffered badly, restrictions returned, and a twenty per cent cut was imposed on factories from 23 September.

According to today's *Dinamalar*, demand yesterday was 13,170 MW while generation was only 12,170 MW, leaving a 1,000-MW shortage. Unannounced residential cuts of two to three hours occurred statewide. Demand is expected to exceed 15,000 MW during the severe heat of April and May, creating a risk of many hours of cuts. What announcements will the AIADMK devise to conceal that danger?

On 25 April 2013, Jayalalithaa read a Rule 110 statement. On 30 April, she read an “explanation” of it. She announced the Rs 7,000-crore, 2,000-MW Sillahalla pumped-storage hydroelectric project in the Nilgiris; power stations and transmission corridors costing Rs 8,000 crore; twenty thousand new transformers costing Rs 500 crore; and fifteen thousand kilometres of power lines costing Rs 850 crore. These schemes, totalling Rs 16,350 crore, would rapidly make Tamil Nadu power-surplus and guarantee uninterrupted, stable supply.
What became of those announcements? Those who opened their eyes wide and cried “Aha! Oh-ho!” when they heard them now stand with their mouths covered.

Apparently believing no one will remember these announcements or question them, today's Chief Minister told the Assembly: “The power shortage has been greatly reduced and we are approaching self-sufficiency in meeting demand.” Former Chief Minister Jayalalithaa, present Chief Minister Panneerselvam and Electricity Minister Viswanathan have repeated this same story for three and a half years, deceiving Tamil Nadu and winding flowers around the people's ears.
How many more days must Tamil Nadu hear this fiction? While the AIADMK remains in power, the story will not end, the power cuts will not disappear and the suffering will not fade.
With affection,
M.K.
23 February 2015
